title: Quantity Surveyor (A&A, Additions & Alterations)
description:
About the Company

Well-established Additions & Alterations (A&A) and interior construction specialist in Singapore, with a strong track record in delivering high-quality upgrading, renovation, and reinstatement works across commercial, residential, retail, and institutional projects.

The company is experienced in managing live operational environments, handling phased construction, tight schedules, and complex stakeholder coordination. With a strong emphasis on safety, quality, and cost control, the organization partners closely with clients, consultants, and subcontractors to deliver projects that meet regulatory requirements and client expectations.

Quantity Surveyor (A&A) to manage cost and contract administration for Additions & Alterations projects, including upgrading works, renovation, reinstatement, and asset enhancement works. The role requires strong cost control, stakeholder coordination, and hands-on exposure to live A&A construction environments.

Pre-Contract / Tender Stage

  • Prepare cost estimates, budgets, and feasibility studies for A&A works
  • Take-off quantities and prepare Bills of Quantities (BQ)
  • Assist in tender documentation, evaluation, and contract award
  • Review drawings and specifications to identify cost implications and risks

Post-Contract / Construction Stage

  • Administer contracts in accordance with SIA / REDAS / PSSCOC forms
  • Prepare and assess progress claims, variation orders (VOs), and final accounts
  • Monitor project costs, cash flow, and budget compliance
  • Evaluate subcontractor claims and manage payments
  • Handle claims, cost reports, and value engineering proposals

A&A-Specific Scope

  • Manage cost implications for phased works, night works, live operational sites, and tight timelines
  • Coordinate with consultants, site teams, subcontractors, and clients on design changes
  • Assess cost impact of unforeseen site conditions common in A&A projects
  • Ensure compliance with BCA regulations, authority submissions, and contract requirements

Stakeholder & Reporting

  • Attend site meetings and coordinate closely with Project Managers and Site Teams
  • Prepare regular cost reports and financial forecasts for management
  • Support audits and internal cost controls

Requirements

  • Diploma / Degree in Quantity Surveying, Building, or Construction Management
  • Minimum 2 years of relevant QS experience in A&A projects
  • Strong knowledge of SIA / PSSCOC contract conditions
  • Experience working with main contractors or A&A specialists
  • Experience in commercial, residential, retail, or institutional A&A works
  • Ability to manage multiple small-to-medium A&A projects concurrently



How to Apply: Click Apply Now — or send your resume directly to Talent@BuildBridgePartners.com
